examples: From 1710, the ruble was divided into 100 kopeks.
The Soviet ruble was called Som in Kazakh, Kyrgyz and Uzbek which are written on the back of the notes.
The strengthening of the ruble sharply increased imports and all the logistics associated with them.
In 1991 the Russian government stole savings out from under mattresses by canceling 50-ruble and 100-ruble notes.
